acronym: massive interests matching engine · flag football

seo.title: MIME-FF · pickup flag football that forms itself
seo.description: a free, open source platform that connects people with the same interest. when enough gather in one area, a game is born.

hero.heading: find your local flag football game, or help it find you
hero.body: while there may not be a standing local game in your area at the moment, don't you think there should be? this site makes that happen.
hero.body2: just let us know your general area, name and email and we'll take care of the rest. when enough people in your area show interest, MIME-FF coordinates and facilitates organic community organized games with little to no effort on your part.

how.title: someone names a spot
how.body: a local suggests place and time.

faq.q: how are game sites determined? who picks them?
faq.a: you or someone else nearby suggests a place, day of week, and time for a regular game. from there the matching engine takes over and does the hard work of figuring out whether a game can come together.

faq.q: who runs the games? who's in charge?
faq.a: no set leader or organizer for a local game, though there may be captains. it does take a little initiative from the people showing up.

captains have no extra duties, just extra abilities - messaging the players, marking a site as "off season." the first captain is whoever suggested the original game. they can make other players captains too.
when you mark that you're coming, you'll see whether anyone else is planning to bring footballs, cones, and flags. if nobody is, grab whatever you can.

faq.q: why show interest before i can see who's around?
faq.a: so the count means something. if anyone could just browse, most people would lurk and the numbers would be noise. show you're in, then you see everyone else who is.

faq.q: what if there aren't enough players near me yet?
faq.a: then there's no game yet, and we won't fake one. you'll see the interest count for your area. once enough people nearby show interest, the engine sparks a game.

faq.q: will a game actually happen, or will everyone flake?
faq.a: you don't just rsvp - you make a soft promise ('if it's on, i'm there'). the game only locks once enough people commit, and the night before everyone gets the roster of who's in.

faq.q: why a weekly standing game instead of one-off events?
faq.a: a standing slot lasts. once it's 'sundays at noon,' nobody renegotiates every week - people just show up. one-offs make you start from zero every time.
it's underserved - plenty of software for youth leagues, almost nothing for adults who just want a regular pickup game. and it's easy to start: small-sided, light on gear, no pads.

faq.q: what do you do with my info?
faq.aHtml: as little as possible. email so we can tell you a game's forming, a display name (a username is fine - no real name needed) so others know who's coming, and your zip to group you with people nearby. never your street address. full details on the privacy page.

free and open source, pay what you can - no paywall, ever. if the app is running your weekly game we ask for a $5/month subscription to keep the servers on, but it's an ask, not a gate. one-time tips help too.

register.seoDescription: tell us where you are and we'll show how many folks nearby want a flag football game going.
register.heading: show interest
register.blurb: tell us where you are. once you're in, you'll see how many folks nearby want the same thing.
register.note: we ask for your email, name, and zip. that's all.

donate.label: support the project
donate.seoDescription: free, open source, pay what you can. a $5/month subscription keeps the servers on, a one-time tip works too, and just showing up for your crew counts.
donate.heading: pay what you can
donate.blurb: free and open source, and it stays that way - no paywall, ever. if the app is running your weekly game, we ask for $5/month to keep the servers on. it's an ask, not a gate: skip it and nothing changes. and there's more than one way to chip in.

methods.name: $5 / month
methods.desc: the soft ask. if this app is managing your weekly game, a fiver a month keeps it alive. cancel anytime, and nothing locks if you don't.

methods.name: one-time tip
methods.desc: rather not commit? throw in whatever, whenever. a quick one-tap thanks if that's more your speed.

methods.name: just show up
methods.desc: money's tight? no problem. the best support is being there - bring a friend, fill a roster, keep your local game alive.

footer.tagline: free, open source pickup flag football. no organizer, no fees.
footer.note: open source · not affiliated with the nfl or nfl flag
